endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/facets#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Adds a new Facet to an object. An object can have more than one facet applied on it.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/apply#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Copies the input published schema, at the specified version, into the Directory with the same name and version as that of the published schema.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/attach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description:
Attaches an existing object to another object. An object can be accessed in two ways:

  1. Using the path

  2. Using ObjectIdentifier
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/policy/attach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Attaches a policy object to a regular object. An object can have a limited number of attached policies.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/index/attach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Attaches the specified object to the specified index.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/attach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Attaches a typed link to a specified source and target object. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/batchread#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Performs all the read operations in a batch.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/batchwrite#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Performs all the write operations in a batch. Either all the operations succeed or none.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory/create#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Creates a Directory by copying the published schema into the directory. A directory cannot be created without a schema.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet/create#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Creates a new Facet in a schema. Facet creation is allowed only in development or applied schemas.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/index#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Creates an index object. See Indexing for more information.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Creates an object in a Directory. Additionally attaches the object to a parent, if a parent reference and LinkName is specified. An object is simply a collection of Facet attributes. You can also use this API call to create a policy object, if the facet from which you create the object is a policy facet.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/create
endpoints.description:
Creates a new schema in a development state. A schema can exist in three phases:

  • Development: This is a mutable phase of the schema. All new schemas are in the development phase. Once the schema is finalized, it can be published.

  • Published: Published schemas are immutable and have a version associated with them.

  • Applied: Applied schemas are mutable in a way that allows you to add new schema facets. You can also add new, nonrequired attributes to existing schema facets. You can apply only published schemas to directories.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet/create#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Creates a TypedLinkFacet. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Deletes a directory. Only disabled directories can be deleted. A deleted directory cannot be undone. Exercise extreme caution when deleting directories.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet/delete#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Deletes a given Facet. All attributes and Rules that are associated with the facet will be deleted. Only development schema facets are allowed deletion.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/delete#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Deletes an object and its associated attributes. Only objects with no children and no parents can be deleted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Deletes a given schema. Schemas in a development and published state can only be deleted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet/delete#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Deletes a TypedLinkFacet. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/index/detach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Detaches the specified object from the specified index.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/detach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Detaches a given object from the parent object. The object that is to be detached from the parent is specified by the link name.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/policy/detach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Detaches a policy from an object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/detach#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Detaches a typed link from a specified source and target object. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory/disable#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Disables the specified directory. Disabled directories cannot be read or written to. Only enabled directories can be disabled. Disabled directories may be reenabled.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory/enable#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Enables the specified directory. Only disabled directories can be enabled. Once enabled, the directory can then be read and written to.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/getappliedschema
endpoints.description: Returns current applied schema version ARN, including the minor version in use.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory/get#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves metadata about a directory.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Gets details of the Facet, such as facet name, attributes, Rules, or ObjectType. You can call this on all kinds of schema facets -- published, development, or applied.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description:
Does the following:

  1. Adds new Attributes, Rules, or ObjectTypes.

  2. Updates existing Attributes, Rules, or ObjectTypes.

  3. Deletes existing Attributes, Rules, or ObjectTypes.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/attributes/get#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves attributes that are associated with a typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/attributes/get#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves attributes within a facet that are associated with an object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/information#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves metadata about an object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/json#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves a JSON representation of the schema. See JSON Schema Format for more information.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/json#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Allows a schema to be updated using JSON upload. Only available for development schemas. See JSON Schema Format for more information.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet/get#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns the identity attribute order for a specific TypedLinkFacet. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/applied
endpoints.description: Lists schema major versions applied to a directory. If SchemaArn is provided, lists the minor version.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/indices#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Lists indices attached to the specified object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/development
endpoints.description: Retrieves each Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of schemas in the development state.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/directory/list
endpoints.description: Lists directories created within an account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet/attributes#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves attributes attached to the facet.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/facet/list#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Retrieves the names of facets that exist in a schema.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/incoming#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of all the incoming TypedLinkSpecifier information for an object. It also supports filtering by typed link facet and identity attributes. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/index/targets#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Lists objects attached to the specified index.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/attributes#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Lists all attributes that are associated with an object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/children#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of child objects that are associated with a given object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/parentpaths#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description:
Retrieves all available parent paths for any object type such as node, leaf node, policy node, and index node objects. For more information about objects, see Directory Structure.

Use this API to evaluate all parents for an object. The call returns all objects from the root of the directory up to the requested object. The API returns the number of paths based on user-defined MaxResults, in case there are multiple paths to the parent. The order of the paths and nodes returned is consistent among multiple API calls unless the objects are deleted or moved. Paths not leading to the directory root are ignored from the target object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/parent#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Lists parent objects that are associated with a given object in pagination fashion.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/policy#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns policies attached to an object in pagination fashion.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/outgoing#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of all the outgoing TypedLinkSpecifier information for an object. It also supports filtering by typed link facet and identity attributes. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/policy/attachment#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns all of the ObjectIdentifiers to which a given policy is attached.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/published
endpoints.description: Lists the major version families of each published schema. If a major version ARN is provided as SchemaArn, the minor version revisions in that family are listed instead.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/tags
endpoints.description: Returns tags for a resource. Tagging is currently supported only for directories with a limit of 50 tags per directory. All 50 tags are returned for a given directory with this API call.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet/attributes#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of all attribute definitions for a particular TypedLinkFacet. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet/list#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of TypedLink facet names for a particular schema. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/policy/lookup#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Lists all policies from the root of the Directory to the object specified. If there are no policies present, an empty list is returned. If policies are present, and if some objects don't have the policies attached, it returns the ObjectIdentifier for such objects. If policies are present, it returns ObjectIdentifier, policyId, and policyType. Paths that don't lead to the root from the target object are ignored. For more information, see Policies.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/publish#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Publishes a development schema with a major version and a recommended minor version.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/facets/delete#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Removes the specified facet from the specified object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/tags/add
endpoints.description: An API operation for adding tags to a resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/tags/remove
endpoints.description: An API operation for removing tags from a resource.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: POST /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/attributes/update#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Updates a given typed link’s attributes. Attributes to be updated must not contribute to the typed link’s identity, as defined by its IdentityAttributeOrder.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/object/update#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Updates a given object's attributes.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/update#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Updates the schema name with a new name. Only development schema names can be updated.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/typedlink/facet#x-amz-data-partition
endpoints.description: Updates a TypedLinkFacet. For more information, see Typed link.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/upgradeapplied
endpoints.description: Upgrades a single directory in-place using the PublishedSchemaArn with schema updates found in MinorVersion. Backwards-compatible minor version upgrades are instantaneously available for readers on all objects in the directory. Note: This is a synchronous API call and upgrades only one schema on a given directory per call. To upgrade multiple directories from one schema, you would need to call this API on each directory.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.

endpoints.name: PUT /amazonclouddirectory/2017-01-11/schema/upgradepublished
endpoints.description: Upgrades a published schema under a new minor version revision using the current contents of DevelopmentSchemaArn.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{AMAZONAWS_API_KEY}} with your AMAZONAWS API KEY.
